jokes.text: Ooh, Jar Jar, everyone hates you but me.
jokes.setup: Homer dreaming, referencing the widely-hated Star Wars character
jokes.punchline: Homer being the only person who likes Jar Jar Binks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer contrarian take on popular culture, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Cool! Mom's on drugs. If we turn her in, we can get a form letter from Dick Cheney.
jokes.setup: Marge appears intoxicated, prompting Bart's observation
jokes.punchline: Bart's mercenary attitude about turning in his own mother for a meaningless reward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cynicism combined with political satire - the specificity of 'form letter from Dick Cheney' elevates it

jokes.text: You know how hard it is to get a doctor on Wednesday morning.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Homer's snoring problem
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Wednesday morning being specifically difficult for doctor appointments
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about medical appointments - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: I haven't lost so much sleep since little Bartie had the scoots.
jokes.setup: Marge discussing her sleep deprivation
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic 'scoots' and 'little Bartie' for what was clearly a messy situation
jokes.explanation: Marge's maternal euphemism is perfectly in character, mild but effective

jokes.text: Here's what it costs. Interesting. Here's my counteroffer.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert shows Homer the cost of surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate rejection and 'counteroffer' (likely showing no money)
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer poverty gag, elevated by the formal 'counteroffer' language

jokes.text: Get out.
jokes.setup: Homer's presumably insulting counteroffer
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's blunt, immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that cap the exchange effectively

jokes.text: Pedals, people!
jokes.setup: Family presumably struggling with some physical activity
jokes.punchline: Marge's frustrated coaching directive
jokes.explanation: Marge's organizational nature under pressure - mild physical comedy

jokes.text: So Lisa, do you have a date for the harvest dance? This is not a good time! It's never a good time!
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Lisa's social life at an inappropriate moment
jokes.punchline: Lisa's escalating frustration about the timing and general pattern
jokes.explanation: Great parent-child dynamic - Marge's oblivious timing vs Lisa's exasperation

jokes.text: Overnight bag, no husband in sight! It's happened!
jokes.setup: Marge arrives at her sisters' with luggage
jokes.punchline: Patty's immediate assumption and excitement about marital problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty - her anti-Homer bias makes her leap to conclusions with glee

jokes.text: She left Homer? I'll get the Champale. And let's get that ring off.
jokes.setup: Selma hearing about potential marital crisis
jokes.punchline: Immediate preparation for celebration with cheap champagne and ring removal
jokes.explanation: Champale (cheap champagne) is perfect class detail, and the immediate ring removal shows how ready they are

jokes.text: Great. We'll have a girls night. No bras!
jokes.setup: Planning a girls' night in
jokes.punchline: Selma's overly enthusiastic and inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Selma's crude attempt at sisterly bonding - uncomfortable but in character

jokes.text: It's a cable show about four single women who act like gay men.
jokes.setup: Explaining what 'Nooky in New York' is about
jokes.punchline: Savage takedown of Sex and the City's writing and character behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural criticism that perfectly captures Sex and the City's tone in one devastating line

jokes.text: If I'm not having sex by the end of this goat cheese quesadilla, I'm gonna scream.
jokes.setup: Parody of Sex and the City dialogue
jokes.punchline: The arbitrary food deadline and pretentious food choice
jokes.explanation: Good parody of Sex and the City's over-the-top sexual dialogue combined with foodie culture

jokes.text: I also enjoy sex.
jokes.setup: Following the previous character's dramatic sex declaration
jokes.punchline: Comically understated and awkward response
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to the previous line - robotic and awkward, great parody timing

jokes.text: Since this morning, I've had sex with a New York Knick, two subway cops and a guy who works on Wall Street. Broker? Nah, she's just really sore.
jokes.setup: Character listing her sexual conquests including Wall Street worker
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'broker' (broke her vs. stock broker) with physical injury implication
jokes.explanation: Solid pun structure with good misdirection - setup makes you expect financial discussion

jokes.text: This is so like our lives. It's like they hid a camera in our apartment.
jokes.setup: Watching the sex-obsessed TV show
jokes.punchline: The sisters identifying with characters having lots of sex
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Patty and Selma's actual dating lives - perfect self-delusion

jokes.text: Gentlemen, start your envy!
jokes.setup: Introducing a list of rich men
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Gentlemen, start your engines' from racing
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that works for the context of rich lists inspiring jealousy

jokes.text: Artie made mega bucks with a revolutionary invention, a converter that changes that horrible modem noise into easy listening music.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Artie got rich
jokes.punchline: Converting annoying modem sounds to pleasant music - absurd but oddly practical
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific tech-era invention that's both absurd and relatable to anyone who lived through dial-up

jokes.text: Also available with lyrics.
jokes.setup: The modem-to-music converter description
jokes.punchline: Adding lyrics to what would be instrumental elevator music makes it even more absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - just when you think the invention can't get more ridiculous

jokes.text: Hey, computer geek / You will be connected in no time
jokes.setup: Example of the lyrics mentioned
jokes.punchline: Actual sung lyrics about dial-up connection
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the setup - hearing the actual absurd lyrics makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, he seemed like a sweet little shrimp. But then he turned into an octopus.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she didn't date Artie seriously
jokes.punchline: Sea creature metaphor for his transformation from innocent to grabby
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for teenage boy behavior - shrimp to octopus captures the transformation beautifully

jokes.text: Artie! Artie, no! Stop! Marge. Marge, you know you can't resist my busy hands. Artie, get off of me!
jokes.setup: Flashback to prom night assault
jokes.punchline: Artie's creepy 'busy hands' line during what's clearly assault
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor - sexual assault played for laughs hasn't aged well, though 'busy hands' is memorably creepy

jokes.text: Are you nuts? E-mail, female.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting writing a letter
jokes.punchline: Email/female pun while embracing modern technology
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that captures early 2000s tech enthusiasm, though somewhat forced

jokes.text: Dear Artie. Dear Hottie. Congratulations on your recent TV appearance. I want to sex you up. Your love slave, Marge.
jokes.setup: Marge dictating innocent message while sisters 'help'
jokes.punchline: Each line gets progressively more inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - shows the sisters' meddling and Marge's horror building

jokes.text: You can't use the word 'sex' on the Internet.
jokes.setup: Marge's reaction to the inappropriate email
jokes.punchline: Her complete misunderstanding of how the Internet works
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety about technology - treats the Internet like broadcast TV

jokes.text: Watch me!
jokes.setup: Marge's innocent concern about Internet decency
jokes.punchline: Selma's defiant attitude about online behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Selma's rebellious nature, simple but effective

jokes.text: A note from Marge Simpson? Well, isn't that a coincidence? I was just thinking about her. For the last 20 years.
jokes.setup: Artie receiving the email, noting the coincidence
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been obsessing over Marge for two decades
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that establishes Artie's creepy obsession - builds from innocent to disturbing

jokes.text: Hey, a helicopter's landing on our lawn. Let's approach with caution.
jokes.setup: Unusual event happening
jokes.punchline: Lisa's measured, rational response to extraordinary situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character work - rational caution in absurd situation

jokes.text: Unguarded breakfasts. The sweetest taboo.
jokes.setup: Artie approaching the Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Treating family breakfast like a forbidden pleasure
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Artie's creepy voyeuristic nature - domestic scenes as erotic fantasy

jokes.text: You can't spell party without Artie, if you misspell party. Or Artie.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to be charming with wordplay
jokes.punchline: The wordplay completely falls apart under examination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the 'you can't spell X without Y' format - Artie's desperation makes him torture language

jokes.text: Was it consummated? Pretty much, yeah.
jokes.setup: Artie asking if Homer's marriage was consummated
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual, understated confirmation
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual attitude toward what should be private information - perfect character stupidity

jokes.text: Homer, don't talk with your mouth full. I've told you before, it's rude!
jokes.setup: Homer just revealed intimate marriage details
jokes.punchline: Marge focusing on table manners instead of the inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge priorities - worried about etiquette while ignoring major social breach

jokes.text: Hey, Marge! It's that guy who couldn't get any off you!
jokes.setup: Bart identifying Artie
jokes.punchline: Crude reduction of their romantic history
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart crudeness that embarrasses everyone - in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's like being married to my best friend, and he lets me feel his boobs.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his marriage to Artie
jokes.punchline: The bizarre addition of the boob-feeling detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer oversharing with unexpected physical detail - wholesome sentiment turned weird

jokes.text: How much sex would be involved? Because if it's some...
jokes.setup: Homer hearing Artie's million-dollar proposal
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate practical question about the sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Homer treating his wife like a business transaction - awful but believably Homer

jokes.text: You'll win her yet, Artie. I mean, Arr-tie!
jokes.setup: Homer encouraging Artie after Marge storms off
jokes.punchline: The pirate pronunciation and treating it like a clever wordplay
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at clever wordplay falls flat - shows his desperation to be helpful

jokes.text: If we had Artie's money, I could get that snoring surgery. Plus at least two of those other surgeries I desperately need.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Marge about the money
jokes.punchline: Revealing he needs multiple other unspecified surgeries
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Homer's poor health - 'at least two' suggests even more problems

jokes.text: Just like I got used to saying 'Courteney Cox-Arquette.'
jokes.setup: Marge saying she'll get used to Homer's snoring
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to accepting another annoying change (actress's hyphenated name)
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that captures the era's hyphenated celebrity names trend - very relatable

jokes.text: One squeaking spring symphony coming up.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she likes some of his bed sounds
jokes.punchline: Homer's poetic description of their broken bed springs
jokes.explanation: Nice euphemism for sex that also highlights their poverty (broken bed springs)

jokes.text: Wow! Homer, that was amazing!
jokes.setup: Post-sex scene
jokes.punchline: Marge's over-the-top enthusiasm (likely sarcastic or Homer's fantasy)
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom post-sex humor - the timing suggests it was very brief or Homer's delusion

jokes.text: Are you snoring while you're awake?
jokes.setup: Homer's snoring problem getting worse
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of snoring while conscious
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's condition - suggests it's gotten impossibly bad

jokes.text: And by funny stuff, I mean hand-holding, goo-goo eyes, misdirected woo, which is pretty much any John Woo film.
jokes.setup: Homer setting ground rules for Artie
jokes.punchline: The progression from innocent to the John Woo pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that builds from romantic warnings to film director pun - good escalation

jokes.text: Your wife's virtues shall remain as untouched as Bill Gates' weight room.
jokes.setup: Artie promising to respect Marge
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Bill Gates' presumably unused gym equipment
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific cultural reference - Gates as stereotypical unathletic tech billionaire

jokes.text: I didn't sell her. I just rented her to an old boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Moe saying Homer sold his wife
jokes.punchline: Homer's distinction between selling and renting makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Homer thinks the rental distinction makes it better when it's arguably worse

jokes.text: That's gonna buy him a lot of swings in the old batting cage.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer's million-dollar payday
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor for sex
jokes.explanation: Standard sports-sex metaphor - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Even after I bought her that hockey fight tape?
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Marge might leave Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking a hockey fight tape was romantic gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion - thinks violent sports compilation is thoughtful romantic gesture

jokes.text: He's like a spy in the house of Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe talking about Artie being stuck in his head
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Spy in the House of Love' with his bar name
jokes.explanation: Decent literary reference adapted for the bar setting - shows Moe's unexpected cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Forwards? No. Backwards! To the sexy '70s!
jokes.setup: Artie promising a trip through time
jokes.punchline: The dramatic revelation that it's going backwards to the '70s
jokes.explanation: Good build-up with the misdirection, though calling the '70s 'sexy' is debatable

jokes.text: I can't believe he's paying us a thousand bucks to pretend it's the '70s. Disco Stu is working pro bono.
jokes.setup: Band members being paid to recreate '70s prom
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu, who always acts like it's the '70s, working for free
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character humor - Disco Stu finally found his perfect gig and works for free out of passion

jokes.text: Tonight you get the prom you always deserved.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining the fake prom concept
jokes.punchline: The implication that Marge's real prom was ruined (by him)
jokes.explanation: Artie's complete lack of self-awareness about why the original prom was bad

jokes.text: The band hasn't worked since the wrap party for James at 16. Some say they're cursed.
jokes.setup: Artie describing the band he hired
jokes.punchline: The specific reference to a forgotten TV show and curse
jokes.explanation: Good specific pop culture reference - James at 16 was a real show, adds authenticity to the joke

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's our high school prom! And once again I have no date!
jokes.setup: Homer crashing the fake prom
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's recreating his dateless prom experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds pathos to Homer's character - explains some of his desperation

jokes.text: And have you been drinking? Just for 25 years.
jokes.setup: Principal asking if Homer's been drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact admission of decades of alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty meets dark reality - the casual delivery makes it funnier and sadder

jokes.text: Very well, Marge. I see you'll never feel about moi the way I feel about toi.
jokes.setup: Artie accepting Marge's rejection
jokes.punchline: Using French pronouns to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie pretentiousness - trying to class up his rejection with bad French

jokes.text: Oh, no! If Marge marries Artie, I'll never be born!
jokes.setup: Bart witnessing his parents' alternate timeline
jokes.punchline: Time travel logic applied to seeing parents with other people
jokes.explanation: Clever application of Back to the Future logic to a family situation - kid's perspective on parents' past

jokes.text: Stop looking at me! Keep dancing!
jokes.setup: Artie after being rejected by Marge
jokes.punchline: Trying to maintain the illusion while falling apart
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Artie's desperation to save face while his plan collapses

jokes.text: Oh, guys, it was horrible. I saw Marge kissing a far superior man.
jokes.setup: Homer returning to the bar after seeing Marge with Artie
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission that Artie is superior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness combined with self-deprecation - he knows he's not the catch

jokes.text: Well, if it makes you feel any better, he's probably doing her right now.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing his pain about losing Marge
jokes.punchline: Moe's attempt at comfort that makes things infinitely worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - his attempt at sympathy backfires spectacularly by making Homer feel worse

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Make me the bad guy.
jokes.setup: Everyone's reaction to Moe's terrible comfort attempt
jokes.punchline: Moe's defensive response to being called out
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe deflection - never takes responsibility for his insensitive comments

jokes.text: Send the bill to Baron Von Kiss-a-lot.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering drinks after his breakdown
jokes.punchline: Silly German-style nickname for Artie
jokes.explanation: Decent nickname construction that captures Homer's petty resentment

jokes.text: Marge, if you're watching this then it means I've figured out how to work the camera.
jokes.setup: Homer's video message to Marge
jokes.punchline: Making his technological incompetence part of the dramatic message
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - even his heartfelt message includes practical considerations about technology

jokes.text: Kiss me, Artie. With pleasure. Homer's a big jerk. Ooh.
jokes.setup: Homer using dolls to recreate what he saw
jokes.punchline: The dolls acting out the scene including insults about Homer
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Homer torturing himself by recreating the scene with dolls

jokes.text: Don't worry about the kids. I'll drop them off with Patty and Selma. Patty and Selma? Screw that!
jokes.setup: Homer's suicide video mentioning childcare arrangements
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate rejection of going to the aunts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction - even Dad's suicide is less important than avoiding the aunts

jokes.text: I carved that one wonderful summer. What did Carl think? You know, we've never discussed it.
jokes.setup: Lenny showing Homer Mount Carlmore
jokes.punchline: The realization that Carl doesn't know about the mountain carved in his honor
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Lenny's awkward devotion to Carl and lack of communication

jokes.text: I'm gonna leave this world the way I entered it, dirty, screaming, and torn away from the woman I love.
jokes.setup: Homer taking a dangerous oil rig job
jokes.punchline: Birth metaphor applied to death with romantic addition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - birth metaphor for death is clever, and adding Marge makes it both funnier and sadder

jokes.text: Quick and pointless. That's the death for me.
jokes.setup: Foreman describing the dangerous work
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for a meaningless death
jokes.explanation: Good dark Homer humor - treating death like a consumer choice with his typical preferences

jokes.text: But West Springfield's three times the size of Texas. We'll never find him there.
jokes.setup: Family trying to locate Homer
jokes.punchline: West Springfield being impossibly large compared to Texas
jokes.explanation: Good Simpsons geography absurdism - Springfield areas defying physical reality

jokes.text: Does that work on anyone? No. But when it does, hello!
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Artie's 'friends with privileges' line
jokes.punchline: Artie admitting it never works but remaining optimistic
jokes.explanation: Good structure with Artie's pathetic optimism despite complete failure rate

jokes.text: Looks like we're goners. Oh, well. Circle of life.
jokes.setup: Facing imminent death on oil rig
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance with Disney reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer fatalism - treats death like a minor inconvenience with pop culture wisdom

jokes.text: Listen to me, Homer! You've won! You own Marge's heart, and that's something I could never buy.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to save Homer
jokes.punchline: Admitting his wealth can't buy love
jokes.explanation: Standard rich-guy-learns-money-can't-buy-love lesson - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I can't take his money. Oh. I can't take his money. I can't print my own money. I have to work for money. Why don't I just lie down and die?
jokes.setup: Marge saying they can't accept Artie's money
jokes.punchline: Homer's escalating despair about his financial limitations
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's economic despair - the progression from noble to pathetic is perfect

jokes.text: Now, Homer, if there's one thing you should've learned from all this, it's that I'm rich, rich, rich! And now, I bid you adieu!
jokes.setup: Artie's supposed lesson for Homer
jokes.punchline: The lesson is just that Artie is very rich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie narcissism - even his moral lesson is about his own wealth

jokes.text: He's a loser, Marge. Dump him! I travel the world and the seven seas I am watching you through a camera!
jokes.setup: Artie spying through the snore converter
jokes.punchline: His desperate final plea combined with admitting he's spying
jokes.explanation: Good final Artie moment showing his stalker tendencies - the Eurythmics reference adds to the creepiness
